name: Collserola Park and Rabassada Climb Loop
name_original: Sant Adriá de Besós, puente de Montcada y Tibidabo  — circular por el  Parque Natural de Collserola
description: This difficult 48.3-mile road cycling route through Collserola Natural Park offers challenging climbs and panoramic views of Barcelona.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santa Coloma de Gramenet offer varied terrain, from the flat paths along the Besòs River to the climbs within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. This region features Mediterranean forests and mountain passes, providing diverse cycling experiences. The landscape includes continuous, moderate complexes and relatively gentle relief, suitable for different fitness levels. Cyclists can find both paved routes and more challenging excursions with elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Santa Coloma de Gramenet The most popular road cycling route is Paseo marítimo de Premia de Mar y río Besós - circular route of Barcelona north, a 40.0 miles (64.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route offers a mix of coastal and riverside cycling. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Collserola Park and Rabassada Climb Loop, a difficult 48.3 miles (77.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Collserola Park. Local road cyclists also love the Maristany Bridge – Coco Beach loop from Trinitat Vella, a 34.8 miles (55.9 km) trail leading through urban and coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Santa Coloma de Gramenet is defined by Mediterranean forests, mountain passes, and the Besòs River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Santa Coloma de Gramenet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 47,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Coloma de Gramenet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Santa Coloma de Gramenet?
A: Road cycling routes in Santa Coloma de Gramenet offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find flat paths along the Besòs River, ideal for relaxed rides, as well as climbs within the &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Parc_de_la_Serralada_de_Marina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc de la Serralada de Marina&lt;/a&gt;. The region features Mediterranean forests and mountain passes, providing continuous, moderate complexes and relatively gentle relief suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Santa Coloma de Gramenet?
A: There are over 160 road cycling routes available around Santa Coloma de Gramenet, catering to different skill levels. This includes approximately 35 easy routes, 79 moderate routes, and 46 difficult routes for those seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Santa Coloma de Gramenet offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. The Besòs River Bike Path and Besòs Riverside Park provide mostly paved, flat loops. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Fountain loop from Santa Coloma&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 33.5 km with moderate elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For challenging rides with significant elevation gains, explore routes that venture into the Parc de la Serralada de Marina or the nearby Collserola Park. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983189012/collserola-park-and-rabassada-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collserola Park and Rabassada Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 77 km and features substantial ascents.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I discover while road cycling here?
A: While cycling, you can enjoy Mediterranean forests, mountain passes, and coastal views. The &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Parc_de_la_Serralada_de_Marina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc de la Serralada de Marina&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic environments with oak and pine trees. Routes often provide panoramic vistas of Barcelona and the coastline, with community tips highlighting spots like the "View of Barcelona from the Serralada de Marina".

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to visit along the routes?
A: Yes, many cycling routes in the vicinity pass by significant historical and cultural sites. You might encounter the Gothic monastery of Sant Jeroni de la Murtra, ancient Iberian settlements like Turó d'en Boscà, or even natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features impressive man-made monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7478049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumental Cascade, Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, circular routes are common. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983214193/paseo-maritimo-de-premia-de-mar-y-rio-besos-circular-route-of-barcelona-north" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo marítimo de Premia de Mar y río Besós - circular route of Barcelona north&lt;/a&gt; offers a mix of coastal and riverside cycling in a loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31364312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maristany Bridge – Coco Beach loop from Trinitat Vella&lt;/a&gt;, which explores urban and coastal areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santa Coloma de Gramenet?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, while winter cycling is often feasible due to the Mediterranean climate, though cooler temperatures and occasional rain should be expected.

Q: How accessible are the road cycling routes by public transport?
A: Santa Coloma de Gramenet is well-connected within the Barcelona metropolitan area, making many routes accessible via public transport. The Besòs River Bike Path, for example, is easily reachable from urban centers. Specific route starting points may vary in their direct public transport links, so checking local transport options for your chosen route is advisable.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: Yes, many routes, especially those passing through urban areas or connecting to coastal towns, offer opportunities for refreshment stops. The region's vibrant communities mean you can often find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel during your ride. Routes within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina might have fewer options, so planning ahead for longer excursions is recommended.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Santa Coloma de Gramenet?
A: The road cycling experience in Santa Coloma de Gramenet is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the flat riverside paths to the challenging climbs, and the beautiful Mediterranean landscapes.

Q: Are there options for longer distance road cycling tours?
A: Yes, Santa Coloma de Gramenet serves as an excellent starting point for longer distance road cycling tours. Routes can extend into the wider Barcelona province, connecting to extensive networks of mountain roads and marked trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983189012/collserola-park-and-rabassada-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collserola Park and Rabassada Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at over 77 km, is an example of a longer, more demanding ride.
tips: Bike lane that runs parallel to the river Besós with good views. Be careful as there are also pedestrians who sometimes ignore the bike lane lines.

The Pont de Montcada connects the city of Montcada and Reixach with the road from Roca del Vallès to Santa Coloma de Gramanet (BV5001). It is a mandatory crossing point for routes bound for Besòs and the beach coming from Vallès.

At the end of a climb you arrive at this fantastic viewpoint from which you can enjoy views of all of Barcelona.

You can also access the Collserola valley and the Carretera de las Aguas.
A classic climb from Barcelona. You can continue along the les aigües road (gravel) or straight ahead to cross Colserolla through Cerdanyola (road).

A large esplanade between Turó d'en Corts and Turó de l'Espinagosa, which was formed by successive dumping of rubble. In 1990 it was conditioned as a viewpoint. It has benches and different stone pines. It has 45 parking spaces. It is a good starting point for itineraries of Sant Pere Mártir.
Picnic area with bar and terrace next to the old hermitage of Santa Creu d'Olorda'. Good place to rest and regain strength.
A nice little cafe in La Floresta. Here you can find coffee, sandwhiches, empanadas, salads, and soups. It has options for vegetarians and vegans.
The best way to go up to Tibidabo. Smooth and undamaged roads. There is a gas station mid way where you can catch your breath.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike tour is approximately 77.7 kilometers long with over 1,590 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 4 hours and 22 minutes to complete, but your actual time may vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions and terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly paved, with about 66.8 km of asphalt and 10.6 km of paved roads, making it largely suitable for racebikes. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride. You'll encounter twisting roads, significant climbs, and exhilarating descents, especially on the well-paved climb to Tibidabo, which features constant gradients of 4-6%.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as a 'Hard road ride' and requires 'very good fitness'. With its substantial distance and over 1,590 meters of elevation gain, it's an ideal challenge for intermediate to advanced cyclists accustomed to climbing. Beginners might find it too demanding, especially the ascent to Tibidabo.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to breathtaking panoramic views of Barcelona and the coastline, especially from the summit of Tibidabo. Key highlights include the Besós River Riverside Path, the View of the Besòs River from the Montcada Bridge, Turó Sul Viewpoint, Cerdanyola Viewpoint, and the stunning View of Barcelona from Turó d'en Cors.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The climbs in Collserola Natural Park are generally rideable year-round. Even in winter, snow at Tibidabo's summit is a rare occurrence. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find options to refuel. One notable spot is Bistró Les Escales. There are also villages within the Collserola Natural Park, like Molins de Rei, where you can find additional services.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for cycling in Parc de Collserola?
faqs.answer: Parc de Collserola is a protected natural park. While cycling is generally permitted on designated routes, it's crucial to stay on marked paths to protect the environment. There are no specific entrance fees for the park itself. Always be mindful of other park users, especially hikers, and respect local wildlife. Specific regulations regarding permits for certain activities or areas might apply, so it's always best to check the official park website for the most current information.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging racebike route, primarily on roads and paved paths. While dogs are generally allowed in Collserola Natural Park, this specific route is not ideal for bringing a dog due to its length, difficulty, and the nature of road cycling. If you plan to bring a dog to the park, ensure they are on a leash and consider shorter, more appropriate trails for walking or hiking.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and elevation, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, and appropriate cycling gear. A repair kit, spare tube, and pump are essential. Sunscreen, sunglasses, and a helmet are also highly recommended. Consider bringing a light jacket, as temperatures can vary, especially at higher elevations like Tibidabo.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find yourself on sections of the Carenejant Collserola, Ronda Verda - Sector Collserola-Pg. de les Aigües, and Catalunya E18, among others, for short stretches.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the starting point by public transport?
faqs.answer: The tour starts in Sant Adriá de Besós. For parking, you would need to find street parking or a public car park in the area. For public transport, Sant Adriá de Besós is well-connected by metro (L2) and tram (T4, T5, T6), making it accessible from various parts of Barcelona. Check local transport schedules for the most convenient option to reach the starting point near the Besós River.
